approaches.rationale: Echo is a single-module pure-Go library (github.com/labstack/echo/v5) with standard package layout and table-driven tests; the native rules_go+Gazelle path with go_deps.from_file should generate library and test targets across the root, middleware/, and echotest/ packages cleanly.
approaches.module_bazel:
module(
    name = "echo",
    version = "5.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_go", version = "0.61.1")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")

go_sdk = use_extension("@rules_go//go:extensions.bzl", "go_sdk")
go_sdk.download(version = "1.25.4")

go_deps = use_extension("@gazelle//:extensions.bzl", "go_deps")
go_deps.from_file(go_mod = "//:go.mod")
approaches.root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")

# gazelle:prefix github.com/labstack/echo/v5
# gazelle:go_naming_convention import_alias
gazelle(name = "gazelle")
anticipated_risks: testdata/_fixture file access in tests requires correct data deps (Gazelle usually handles testdata dirs)
external test packages (_external_test.go) and benchmark tests should be handled by Gazelle but may need verification
TLS/network-dependent tests (server_test.go) may fail under sandbox even though they compile
